note: Illustrative machine-readable noetic-orthing episode (Decision 0021). SYNTHETIC and creed-internal-analysis-scoped; NOT empirical, NOT a real person, NOT a soul-state claim. Types follow the daee crosswalk; symbols are namespaced (D.*).

occurrence.description: a single written objection to a claim, considered at one moment

declared_noetic_analysis.school_boundary: explicitly Athari/Taymiyyan, creed-internal; NOT school-neutral; NOT empirical
declared_noetic_analysis.profile_space: candidate noetic state-types under this declared analysis (deformation repertoire as CANDIDATES, not truths)

observation.public_discourse: the objection's wording, cited context, and source markers only
observation.not_available: the writer's actual interior condition (never read from surface discourse)

inferred_profile.uncertainty: high; the wording does not discriminate among the candidates
inferred_profile.is_not: NOT O*(m;A); NOT ground truth; NOT soul access; NOT a motive verdict

source_warrant_states.note: kept as separate evidence/warrant channels, not one scalar

diagnostic_ir.is_not: NOT one metaorthemma; NOT ground truth

executor.self_application: audited by the same architecture (evaluator symmetry)

policy_ttp.rationale: underdetermined inferred profile => no moralized placement

claim_ledger.claim: an admissible criterion governs this objection
claim_ledger.non_claim: no assertion about the writer's motive or soul-state

residuals_holds.burden: obtain a discriminating observation to separate the candidate profiles

post_render_reread.reason: a load-bearing candidate-discrimination burden remains live

explicit_non_claims: no claim about the writer's motive
no claim about the writer's interior soul-state
no claim the writer accepted or was restored (runtime closure only)
no empirical claim; no validation of the analysis's own premises
